name: reverse-engineering-repo-research
description: Research the strongest reverse-engineering repos for a target need, evaluate them through reverse-engineering and repo-ingestion panels, and extract an EVOKORE-ready operating model.

target_system.description: Target system or MCP environment the extracted patterns should fit

evaluation_depth.description: How deeply to inspect repos: quick, standard, deep

include_meta_improvement.description: Run optional meta-improvement after the workflow completes

research_report.description: Curated reverse-engineering repo report with extracted workflow patterns and EVOKORE adoption recommendations

inputs.instruction: Parse the reverse-engineering need into structured criteria. Separate the search into static analysis, debugger/dynamic, automation/emulation, malware triage, and language-specific decompiler lanes. Generate GitHub and community search queries for each lane.
Execute GitHub searches across all reverse-engineering lanes. Capture stars, forks, recent activity, language, topics, and what part of the workflow each repo strengthens.
Scan books, docs, conference talks, plugin ecosystems, and community recommendations to identify repos that matter operationally even if GitHub search ranking under-represents them.

Deduplicate and cluster all candidates by workflow lane: static platform, dynamic debugger, automation/emulation, malware/capability triage, language-specific decompiler, and semantic recovery. Rank for complementarity rather than redundant popularity.

For each shortlisted repo, inspect README/docs and extract: workflow stages supported, plugin or scripting model, evidence/reporting ergonomics, automation hooks, debugger/decompiler interplay, and unique strengths worth adopting into EVOKORE.

Convene the Reverse Engineering Panel. Review the evaluated repos as a portfolio. Decide which repos best cover static analysis, dynamic validation, automation, malware triage, and semantic recovery. Extract additive workflow patterns, not one-to-one tool clones.

Convene the Repo Ingestion Panel. Validate that the extracted recommendations are adoptable, accurately represented, and low enough risk for EVOKORE integration. Flag repos or patterns that should remain reference-only.

Turn the portfolio review into an EVOKORE-native operating model: expert personas, workflows, skills, memory loops, and adoption strategy. Separate direct extraction targets from reference-only inspirations.

Run the meta-improvement cycle against this reverse-engineering research pass. Evaluate the panel composition, workflow coverage, and whether the extracted operating model still has missing expert lenses.
